title: Generated SSH key uses PKCS#8 PEM — OpenSSH rejects it as invalid format
description: generateKeypair() in ssh-connection-service.ts emits the private key as PKCS#8 PEM (-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----). OpenSSH refuses to load it: 'Load key: invalid format'. Need to emit native OpenSSH format (-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----) which is what ssh-keygen produces and OpenSSH always accepts. Node has no built-in OpenSSH export, but the raw 32-byte components are available via JWK export ('d' = priv, 'x' = pub).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #239. generateKeypair now emits native OpenSSH private key format (-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----) by hand-encoding the openssh-key-v1 wire format. Verified live with ssh-keygen.

title: SSH plugin drops shellArgs — sessions run bare 'ssh'
description: The SSH plugin returns shellCommand='ssh' + shellArgs=[...] separately, but session-service.ts:639 only uses shellCommand. tmux runs bare 'ssh' which prints usage and exits. Fix: combine into a single shell-quoted string in shellCommand (matching agent plugin's buildAgentCommand convention). Reproduced in production after PR #233 deploy.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #238 (merged). SSH plugin now combines all args into a single shell-quoted shellCommand string, matching the agent plugin convention. Bare 'ssh' bug fixed; sessions now connect properly.

title: Non-tmux session resume returns 410 → client auto-deletes singleton tabs
description: Smoking-gun repro captured in user's dev logs: POST /api/sessions/{id}/resume returns 410 for non-tmux terminal-type sessions (settings/profiles/recordings/prefs/secrets). title: Mobile terminal disappears on Pixel Fold unfold (MobileShell strips height wrapper above 768px)
description: When a UA-mobile device (e.g. Pixel 10 Pro Fold) unfolds, viewport crosses 768px. MobileApp keeps rendering MobileSessionView (UA-based useMobile is true), but MobileShell's pass-through path (useIsMobileViewport false) strips its 'h-[100dvh] flex flex-col' wrapper. title: mobile/terminal: adversarial-review fixes for touch selection
description:
Adversarial review of the touch-interactions hook surfaced four high-confidence bugs:

1. Selection rows are viewport-relative but xterm.select() expects buffer-absolute rows. When user has scrolled scrollback, long-press on viewport row N selects buffer row N — usually off-screen content, not what the user touched. Fix: add terminal.buffer.active.viewportY to row computed from viewport coords for both start and end of selection.

2. Multi-touch landing during selection invalidates state but leaves the xterm visual selection painted. reset() doesn't call terminal.clearSelection(), so selection persists visually while mode flips to idle and other handlers (scroll, pinch) take over. Fix: when reset() runs while there's an active selection, also call terminal.clearSelection().

3. The Terminal.touch-scroll.test.ts 'ignores multi-touch' test constructs an event but never invokes handlers.handleTouchMove on it. Test passes trivially without exercising the bail-out path. Fix: actually invoke the handler.

4. handleTouchEnd resets state to idle whenever ANY finger lifts, even if other fingers are still down. A two-finger gesture where one finger lifts will then leave the remaining finger unable to scroll/tap/long-press because mode is idle. Fix: only reset when e.touches.length === 0; otherwise leave state and let the remaining gesture continue.
close_reason: Merged via PR #230.

title: mobile/terminal: selection-vs-scroll, tap not reaching TUI, tap doesn't deselect
description:
Follow-up bugs from PR #227:

1. Selection drag is fighting scroll. When long-press has activated selection mode, subsequent vertical finger movement also triggers the touch-scroll handler, so the user can't extend the selection up/down without the viewport scrolling. The interactions touchmove listener is currently passive — it can't preventDefault, and the scroll handler isn't aware of selection mode.

2. Tap doesn't reach the TUI as a click, AND doesn't scrollToBottom either. User reports neither path fires. Possible causes: (a) appOwnsMouse detection always reports true so scrollToBottom is gated off, (b) the synthesized mousedown/mouseup target lookup fails or the events don't reach xterm's actual handler, (c) the tap-detection thresholds reject real taps.

3. Tapping outside an active selection does not clear it. Standard mobile UX: tap clears any existing selection and does not fire the click that turn (or does, depending on platform — pick one).

Fix:
- Make the interactions touchmove listener {passive: false} so it can preventDefault when mode === selection. Coordinate with touch-scroll.ts: either share state through a mode flag, or have one handler run first and short-circuit the other.
- Investigate tap path. Verify dispatch target with a real browser. Verify appOwnsMouse logic. Add a fallback: if tap with no active selection, ALWAYS scrollToBottom in addition to dispatching the synthetic click — the click is harmless when mouse mode is off, and scrollToBottom should be the user's expected fallback.
- On tap with terminal.hasSelection() === true, call terminal.clearSelection() and skip the click synthesis. The next tap goes through normally.
close_reason: Merged via PR #228.

title: mobile: replace ansi-to-html <pre> output with xterm.js
description:
MobileSessionView renders WebSocket data via ansi-to-html appended to a growing <pre> block. This breaks all TUIs (Claude Code, vim, etc.) because cursor moves, line clears, and screen redraws stack instead of overwriting. Symptom: garbled overlapping characters in any agent session.

Fix: Render terminal output via TerminalWithKeyboard (the same xterm.js wrapper desktop uses). Keep MobileSessionView chrome (SessionStatusBar, banner, SmartKeyStrip, MobileInputBar, SessionMetadataSheet, AgentExitScreen, AuthErrorOverlay). Drop the ansi-to-html pipeline, output entries state, append-only rendering, and useViewportDimensions (xterm + FitAddon already handles dimensions).

Acceptance:
- Claude Code, vim, htop render correctly on mobile
- Pinch-to-zoom still adjusts font size and persists
- prefs.fontFamily / fontSize honored on mount
- SmartKeyStrip + MobileInputBar still drive sendInput
- Suspended/disconnected/reconnecting banners still show
close_reason: Merged via PR #224.

title: Mobile session view: terminal doesn't resize, ignores font preferences
description: MobileSessionView hardcodes MOBILE_COLS=120, MOBILE_ROWS=24 and never calls sendResize, so the PTY thinks the terminal is 120 cols regardless of phone viewport. Lines wrap visually mid-stream, breaking TUI layouts. Also fontFamily is hardcoded to JetBrainsMono Nerd Font Mono — desktop reads prefs.fontFamily from PreferencesContext (SessionManager.tsx:2137-2138). Mobile must do the same. Fix: (a) compute cols/rows from viewport width / charWidth(fontSize) and viewport height / lineHeight, (b) wire ResizeObserver + fontSize change to call sendResize, (c) read prefs.fontFamily and use it for the pre block style + measurement, (d) consider seeding initial fontSize from prefs.fontSize when no localStorage key set.
close_reason: Merged via PRs #222 and #223.

title: Couple projects strictly to folders
description: Major refactor: strictly couple folder and project. Parent folders become groupings only; child folders are projects. Remove decoupled project abstraction.
design: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-project-folder-refactor-master.md — master plan with all shared decisions (naming, hierarchy, ownership matrix, groups-as-active rollup, migration strategy, risk register).
notes:
DECISIONS (2026-04-20):
- Hierarchy: Groups nestable (any depth), Projects are leaves only
- Ownership: Groups hold preferences only. Projects own sessions, tasks, channels, secrets, git, profile, account binding
- Migration: Auto-classify by depth + contents. Leaf folders -> projects; non-leaf -> groups. Sessions on group-like folders move to auto-created 'Default' project
- Naming: Full rename. New tables project_group + project. Types Project/Group. APIs /api/projects + /api/groups. FK renames (folderId -> projectId on task/channel/etc)
Master plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-project-folder-refactor-master.md (updated with codex review findings: forward-only cutover policy, Section 6a column appendix, 8-risk register, phase-specific integration tests)

title: Defer desktop-only context providers off mobile critical path (close mobile perf to >=90)
description:
gj45 shipped MobileViewportSwitch code-split (PR #242), bringing mobile / from 71 to 84 perf and unused-JS from 366 KB to 99 KB. Mobile target (>=90) still missed by 6 points. Root cause: src/app/page.tsx eagerly imports 18 context providers (SessionContext, ProjectTreeContext, GitHubStatsContext, GitHubIssuesContext, SessionMCPContext, BeadsContext, ScheduleContext, etc). Many are desktop-only — their dependency graphs (and their own initialization effects) ship in the mobile bundle.

Fix sketch: split the provider tree into a 'core' set (Preferences, Session, Notification, Channel, Profile — what mobile actually uses) and a 'desktop-only' set wrapped behind the next/dynamic boundary so it lives in the desktop chunk. Some providers may need their internal eager imports (e.g. xterm-related modules in SessionMCPContext) lazy-loaded too.

Acceptance: Lighthouse mobile / >=90 perf, with no regression on desktop perf or feature behavior. Compare against docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile-gj45/home-mobile.report.json baseline.

title: Phase 7 perf: code-split MobileViewportSwitch desktop branch off the mobile critical path
description:
Lighthouse mobile measurement (k583) shows the / route on mobile scoring 72-73, with a single 470 KB JS chunk that's 72% unused (340 KB). Root cause: MobileViewportSwitch renders both branches in the same client component tree, so the desktop SessionManager dependency graph (xterm, codemirror, sidebars, modals, ...) ships in the initial page bundle even on small viewports.

Fix sketch:
- Wrap the desktop branch with next/dynamic import + ssr:false (or ssr:true with loading) so mobile viewports never download the desktop bundle.
- Mirror for the mobile branch on desktop.
- Re-run Lighthouse from docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile against this branch and verify perf >=90 on both viewports.
- Watch CLS - make sure the swap doesn't cause layout jump above 0.1.

Acceptance: perf >=90 on Lighthouse mobile / and Lighthouse desktop /. unused-javascript savings on / drop below 100 KB.

Reference: docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile/README.md (k583 measurement run).
close_reason: MobileViewportSwitch code-split shipped via PR #242. Mobile / 71->84 perf, desktop / 64->92 perf, mobile unused-JS 366KB->99KB (acceptance criterion #3 met). Mobile perf target (>=90) not fully met (84) — remaining gap is the eagerly-imported desktop-only context providers in app/page.tsx. The bead's stated scope (code-split MobileViewportSwitch) is complete; remaining provider-deferral work split out into c9aq for separate scoping. tx71 (login bundle trim) remains open as separate issue.

title: Re-assert client_focus on WS reconnect; ARIA on claim pill
description:
Adversarial review of PR #229 (remote-dev-nlfm) surfaced two valid concerns to address before merge:

1. **Reconnect/server-restart focus gap**: After ws.onopen, the client only sends client_focus on the next OS-level focus event. If the window already had focus when the WS reconnected, no event fires and the server never learns this connection is the active one. Fix: in ws.onopen, proactively send client_focus if document.hasFocus() && !document.hidden.

2. **Claim pill a11y**: The 'Another window is in control · click to claim' button has descriptive text but should announce its appearance to screen readers and have a clear aria-label so the action is obvious in isolation.

Other adversarial review claims (invariant violations, tmux thrash race, iOS visibilitychange unreliability, tiling WM focus issues) did not hold up under code-tracing — the server's single source of truth on sessionPrimaryConnection prevents the claimed races.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #229 (merged)

title: Auto-resize terminal on tab/window focus with multi-client priority
description:
Currently, terminals only resize when the user toggles the sidebar or switches sessions. When switching between mobile and desktop clients (or two windows of the same session), the terminal stays mis-sized until manual interaction.

Root cause: server's primary-connection policy is 'newest connection wins' rather than 'most recently focused'. After mobile attaches, desktop's resize messages no longer call tmux resize-window.

Solution: 
- Add focus/blur listeners on client (currently only visibilitychange exists)
- Server tracks lastFocusAt + isVisible per connection
- Promotion to primary based on last-focused, with 1s cooldown to prevent ping-pong
- Subtle UI indicator + manual claim button when this client is not primary
- Stale takeover: if primary has been hidden, new visible client claims immediately
notes: Implemented in PR #229 — https://github.com/btli/remote-dev/pull/229
close_reason: Shipped via PR #229 (merged)

title: mobile/terminal: tap-to-click + long-press-to-select on xterm.js
description:
Two missing mobile interactions in the xterm.js viewport:

1. Single-tap should deliver a click to the TUI (when the TUI has enabled application mouse mode via DECSET 1000/1006). Today only scroll works because usePinchZoom calls preventDefault on touchstart, suppressing the browser's synthetic mouse-event chain that xterm.js listens to. Use case: tapping a button/option in Claude Code's TUI; tapping the bottom of the buffer to scroll-to-bottom (terminals interpret a click on the prompt line that way).

2. Long-press + drag should select text. xterm.js exposes selection via term.select() / term.getSelection() but the canvas isn't natively selectable on iOS. Plan: detect long-press on the terminal element (~500ms hold without movement), enter a custom selection mode, and on subsequent touchmove translate finger position to (col, row) cells and update selection via term.select() or by synthesizing the mouse-drag events xterm internally listens to.

Location: src/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx (xterm wrapper) and/or src/components/terminal/TerminalWithKeyboard.tsx (mobile chrome). usePinchZoom (src/components/mobile/session/usePinchZoom.ts) needs to single-finger pass through touches it can't consume.
close_reason: Merged via PR #227.

title: mobile: fontSize/fontFamily race in MobileSessionView lazy useState seed
description:
MobileSessionView seeds fontSize via lazy useState initializer at first mount: initialFontSize (from useSyncExternalStore in MobileApp) ?? currentPreferences.fontSize (from async PreferencesContext) ?? DEFAULT_FONT_SIZE. On the first client render both upstream values can be undefined (SSR snapshot returns undefined; preferences fetch is in flight), so the seed falls through to DEFAULT_FONT_SIZE and is never reconciled when the real values arrive. The desktop equivalent (PR #223) was fixed via post-init reconciliation inside Terminal.tsx, but that fix only catches the xterm-init window — the upstream freeze in MobileSessionView keeps the prop stale, so reconciliation reapplies the wrong value.

Fix: one-shot reconciliation effect in MobileSessionView that fires when the initial 'undefined→real' hydration of initialFontSize / currentPreferences.fontSize completes. After that, pinch-to-zoom owns the state (preserve the no-resseed-from-prefs invariant once the user has interacted).
close_reason: Merged via PR #226.

title: mobile: swipe-to-dismiss session + clear-all notifications button
description:
Two mobile UX gaps:

1. No way to dismiss/close a session via swipe. Currently swipe-left only suspends, which keeps the session alive. Users want a destructive close action via gesture.

2. Notifications tab has no clear-all button. Per-row swipe-to-delete works but bulk clear requires opening each one.

Fix:
- Two-stage swipe-left on session rows (iOS Mail pattern): short threshold commits Suspend (existing behavior), deeper threshold switches affordance to Close and commits close on release. Suspended sessions: swipe-left = Close (suspend N/A). Use destructive styling on the deeper affordance.
- NotificationsTab header gets a 'Clear all' text button that calls existing deleteAllNotifications(), shown only when there are notifications. Confirm via destructive AlertDialog or toast undo (project pattern is undo via toast — match that).
close_reason: Merged via PR #225.

title: Desktop terminal: fontSize prop not applied on initial mount (race with PreferencesContext)
description: Terminal.tsx initializes xterm with fontSize from fontSizeRef.current (Terminal.tsx:408), seeded by useRef(fontSize) at line 255. The fontSize prop comes from prefs.fontSize via SessionManager.tsx:2137 which is async-loaded by PreferencesContext. On initial mount, fontSize is the default 14; then prefs loads and the font-update effect (Terminal.tsx:1021) fires — but if xtermRef.current isn't set yet (terminal still initializing), it bails. After that the effect never re-fires (same deps), so the terminal stays at the default size until session switch tears down/rebuilds. Fix: either (a) extend the init effect to wait for prefs (or re-apply fontSize/fontFamily inside the init flow once xterm is created and ready), or (b) make the font-update effect detect 'newly initialized' and apply pending font changes.
close_reason: Merged via PRs #222 and #223.

title: Make Global section indent consistent with root groups/projects
description:
Two related code-consistency issues at the root of the project tree:

1. SessionRow uses inline marginLeft for depth indent. ProjectRow and GroupRow both use inline paddingLeft (which overrides Tailwind px-2's padding-left because of inline-style specificity). The two approaches produce visually different indents because px-2's 8px padding-left still applies on top of marginLeft, but paddingLeft overrides it.

2. The GLOBAL section header in ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx is rendered inline (not via GroupRow). It uses className 'gap-1.5 px-2 py-1' with NO inline paddingLeft. GroupRow at depth=0 uses 'paddingLeft: 0px' inline to override px-2's left padding. As a result GLOBAL's chevron is 8px right of Workspace's chevron at depth=0 — they don't align.

Fix: 
- Refactor SessionRow indent to use paddingLeft (consistent with sibling rows), preserving current visual offsets via correct base values (8 for non-reserve, 18 for reserve).
- Add 'paddingLeft: 0px' inline to the GLOBAL header in ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx so it matches GroupRow at depth=0.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #208 (merged → e60757f): SessionRow indent uses paddingLeft like sibling rows; GLOBAL header has inline paddingLeft:0 to match GroupRow at depth=0.

title: Fix Ports row indent alignment in Global section
description:
The 'Ports' singleton session row in the GLOBAL section of the project tree is misaligned: its icon sits ~8px further right than sibling project icons at the same depth.

Root cause: SessionRow.tsx:154 uses marginLeft for indent (chevronReservePx=18 = chevron 12 + gap 6), then className adds px-2 (8px padding-left). Total icon offset = depth*12 + 18 + 8 = depth*12 + 26.

ProjectRow/GroupRow at the same depth use paddingLeft (which overrides px-2's left padding due to inline-style specificity), so their icon is at depth*12 + 18.

Mismatch = 8px in reserve-chevron-space mode.

Fix: subtract 8 from reserve compensation OR switch to paddingLeft. Minimal fix: change 18 → 10.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #207 (merged → c6cf781): SessionRow chevronReservePx 18 → 10 to cancel out px-2 padding-left, aligning Global-section session icons with sibling project row icons.

title: Fix Ports global panel alignment and add close option

title: Terminal: bottom row clipped after resize (WebGL cell-height overshoot)
description: After the settle-and-fit fix (#181), the very last row is still clipped on resize — visible in Claude Code's bottom mode indicator. Caused by xterm's FitAddon computing rows with a fractional cellHeight that the WebGL renderer rounds up when painting, so N rows overflow container height by subpixels. Fix: after fit(), check if rendered screenElement height exceeds container height; if so, terminal.resize(cols, rows-1).

title: Write integration tests for environment persistence
description:
Create integration tests to verify environment persistence across scenarios.

Files: test/integration/environment-persistence.test.ts (NEW)

Test Scenarios:

1. Environment survives shell exit:
   - Create session with profile env
   - Exit shell (send 'exit' command)
   - Reconnect to session
   - Verify environment variables still present

2. Agent restart preserves environment:
   - Create agent session with API keys
   - Send agent exit (or crash it)
   - Restart agent via restart_agent handler
   - Verify API keys still in environment

3. Profile isolation works without breaking HOME:
   - Create session with profile
   - Verify user's .bashrc loaded (echo $BASH_VERSION)
   - Verify CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR points to profile
   - Verify HOME still points to real home

4. Runtime port conflicts detected:
   - Start a server on port 3000
   - Create session with PORT=3000
   - Verify runtime conflict warning returned

5. Environment stack merges correctly:
   - Create folder env with VAR1=folder
   - Create profile with VAR1=profile, VAR2=profile
   - Verify VAR1=folder (folder wins), VAR2=profile

Test Infrastructure:
- Use real tmux sessions (not mocked)
- Create/cleanup sessions in beforeEach/afterEach
- Use test-specific session names (rdv-test-*)

Tests: This IS the test task, should pass when done
close_reason: Created comprehensive integration tests in src/integration/environment-persistence.test.ts covering: (1) tmux session-level env persistence, (2) TmuxEnvironment merge with precedence, (3) ProfileIsolation XDG paths without HOME override, (4) runtime port conflict detection with real TCP servers, (5) environment stack merging. All 18 tests pass.

title: Create RestartAgentUseCase
description:
Create a use case for restarting agent sessions with environment preservation.

File: src/application/use-cases/session/RestartAgentUseCase.ts

Responsibilities:
- Restart agent CLI without losing environment
- Leverage tmux environment persistence (no re-injection needed if tmux session preserved)
- If tmux session recreated, re-inject environment via EnvironmentManager
- Update agent state in repository
- Send new agent command via tmux gateway

Flow:
1. Load session from repository
2. Mark as restarting, save
3. If tmux session exists, just send new command (env persists)
4. If tmux session gone, recreate with environment from EnvironmentManager
5. Mark as running, save

Interface:
export class RestartAgentUseCase {
  constructor(
    sessionRepo: SessionRepository,
    tmuxGateway: TmuxGateway,
    environmentManager: EnvironmentManager
  )
  
  async execute(sessionId: string, userId: string): Promise<Session>;
}

Tests: Mock dependencies, verify state transitions, verify command sent

title: Create ProfileIsolation value object
description:
Create a value object for XDG-compliant profile isolation WITHOUT overriding HOME.

File: src/domain/value-objects/ProfileIsolation.ts

Responsibilities:
- Generate XDG environment variables for profile isolation
- Preserve user's real HOME (don't override)
- Generate agent-specific config paths (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, CODEX_HOME, etc.)
- Generate GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL path
- Validate SSH key paths for GIT_SSH_COMMAND

Interface:
- static create(profileDir: string, realHome: string): ProfileIsolation
- toEnvironment(): TmuxEnvironment
- getConfigHome(): string
- getDataHome(): string  
- getCacheHome(): string
- getGitConfigPath(): string

The key insight: Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME instead of HOME override. This lets agents use isolated configs while user's dotfiles (.bashrc, .zshrc) still load from real HOME.

title: Phase 7 perf: prepare worktree-friendly build path (so Lighthouse measurement is feasible)
description:
Discovered while attempting remote-dev-k583 (Lighthouse mobile perf). Production builds in agent worktrees fail because:

1. Worktrees do not have node_modules — main repo's node_modules cannot be symlinked because Turbopack 16.1 rejects symlinks pointing outside the worktree filesystem root ("Symlink node_modules is invalid, it points out of the filesystem root").
2. Running 'bun install' in a fresh worktree is extremely slow on this machine (>9 minutes and not done), making in-worktree builds time-prohibitive for measurement tasks.
3. The CLAUDE.md rule mandates work happens in worktrees, but the sandbox correctly blocks writes to the main repo.

Options to investigate:
- Allow agents to run 'bun run build' in main checkout when explicitly measuring perf (carve-out in CLAUDE.md or sandbox config).
- Use a shared 'pnpm/bun store' linker mode that produces a real (non-symlinked) node_modules tree quickly.
- Pre-warm node_modules in worktree creation script.
- Switch perf measurement workflow to run on a dedicated branch in main repo instead of worktree.

Acceptance: there is a documented, < 5 min path from a fresh agent worktree to 'bun run build' succeeding.
close_reason: Shipped scripts/worktree-warm.sh in PR #240. APFS cp -cR clone of main repo's node_modules; rsync fallback; bun install fallback. End-to-end cold start: 43s (under 5-min target). Documented in CLAUDE.md. Unblocks k583.

description: Phase 7 a11y verification was completed (PR #234), but Lighthouse perf was not measured because dev-server scores aren't representative of prod. Acceptance: perf >=90 on the main mobile routes, or fixes filed for any sub-90 routes.
notes: Unblocked by unpj. Use ./scripts/worktree-warm.sh to bootstrap node_modules in worktree, then bun run build && bun run start on alternate ports (e.g. 8001/8002), then run Lighthouse.

Trivial fixes did not apply; bundle-size root cause needs invasive code-split, correctly scoped to gj45.

title: mobile/terminal: address skipped items from adversarial review
description:
Address the five items intentionally skipped from PR #230's review:

1. navigator.vibrate inside async setTimeout: not a user-gesture context on some browsers (Android Chrome may suppress). Make the call defensively wrapped or drop it.

2. Distance-metric mismatch: useTouchInteractions cancels long-press at hypot > 5, touch-scroll activates at |dy| > 5. Diagonal swipe (dx=4, dy=4 → hypot 5.66) cancels long-press but doesn't activate scroll → dead zone. Align the metrics or thresholds.

3. xterm private-API fragility: readDims relies on _core._renderService.dimensions.css.cell. If xterm renames it on a minor bump, readDims silently returns null and selection no-ops with no feedback. Emit a one-time console.warn when fallback path is hit so the breakage is visible.

4. Synthetic MouseEvent missing screenX/screenY: real Touch→Mouse synthesis includes screenX/Y. Some xterm code paths read them. Add screenX = clientX + window.scrollX, screenY = clientY + window.scrollY (or just match clientX/Y for simplicity since terminals don't care about screen coords).

5. Frozen selection anchor on keyboard slide-up: review argued the anchor at fire-time origin is correct (user pressed THAT cell, the cell didn't move in buffer space when the keyboard popped up — the element repositioned, not the buffer). Confirm by reading the code and either documenting the design choice in a comment or fixing if the analysis was wrong.
close_reason: Already addressed on master via commit 316dbd7 (PR #231). Bead state was stale. All 5 review items are fixed and have regression tests in src/components/terminal/useTouchInteractions.test.ts.

description: typeMetadataPatch writes are fire-and-forget. Rapid consecutive calls (user clicking fast between issues/PRs/tabs) can race on the server and leave stale metadata. Fix options: single-flight guard per session id on the client, monotonic revision stamps, or server-side last-write-wins with a version check. Reviewer flagged this as design debt (not blocking merge). Current built-ins handle it with the optimistic+reconcile pattern which is 'good enough' but not race-proof.

title: sessionUsesTmux async probe fragility
description: session-service.ts sessionUsesTmux() calls plugin.createSession(syntheticInput) with synchronous fallback to true on Promises. All current built-ins return synchronous SessionConfig objects, but adding any I/O to a plugin's createSession would silently break tmux-skip for that plugin. Fix: extract useTmux into a separate static/declarative flag on TerminalTypeServerPlugin (e.g., useTmux: boolean | ((input) => boolean)) instead of probing via createSession.
